instruction:
Create a complete custom systemd service that runs a health-check script on a timer. (1) Write /usr/local/bin/healthcheck.sh — it should append the line 'health_ok <current UTC datetime>' to /var/log/healthcheck.log and exit 0. Make the script executable. (2) Create /etc/systemd/system/healthcheck.service — Type=oneshot, ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/healthcheck.sh, Description='System Health Check'. (3) Create /etc/systemd/system/healthcheck.timer — OnCalendar=*:0/10 (every 10 minutes), Persistent=true, Unit=healthcheck.service, Description='Health Check Timer'. (4) Reload systemd daemon, enable and start the timer. (5) Manually run the service once so that /var/log/healthcheck.log contains at least one 'health_ok' line. (6) Create /etc/healthcheck.conf with content 'enabled=true
interval=10m'.
source: 外部参考来源：Ubuntu manpage - systemctl(1) (https://manpages.ubuntu.com/manpages/jammy/en/man1/systemctl.1.html)；Ubuntu manpage - systemd.service(5) (https://manpages.ubuntu.com/manpages/jammy/en/man5/systemd.service.5.html)；Ubuntu manpage - systemd.timer(5) (https://manpages.ubuntu.com/manpages/jammy/en/man5/systemd.timer.5.html)

parameters.command: echo {CLIENT_PASSWORD} | sudo -S bash -c 'systemctl stop healthcheck.timer 2>/dev/null; systemctl disable healthcheck.timer 2>/dev/null; rm -f /etc/systemd/system/healthcheck.service /etc/systemd/system/healthcheck.timer /usr/local/bin/healthcheck.sh /var/log/healthcheck.log /etc/healthcheck.conf; systemctl daemon-reload'

command: import pyautogui; import time; time.sleep(0.5); pyautogui.click({SCREEN_WIDTH_HALF}, {SCREEN_HEIGHT_HALF}); time.sleep(0.5); pyautogui.hotkey('ctrl', 'alt', 't'); time.sleep(0.5)

result.command: script_ok='false'; [ -x /usr/local/bin/healthcheck.sh ] && grep -q 'health_ok' /usr/local/bin/healthcheck.sh && script_ok='true'; svc_ok='false'; [ -f /etc/systemd/system/healthcheck.service ] && grep -q 'Type=oneshot' /etc/systemd/system/healthcheck.service && grep -q 'ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/healthcheck.sh' /etc/systemd/system/healthcheck.service && svc_ok='true'; timer_ok='false'; [ -f /etc/systemd/system/healthcheck.timer ] && grep -q 'OnCalendar' /etc/systemd/system/healthcheck.timer && grep -qi 'Persistent=true' /etc/systemd/system/healthcheck.timer && timer_ok='true'; timer_active='false'; systemctl is-active healthcheck.timer >/dev/null 2>&1 && timer_active='true'; timer_enabled='false'; systemctl is-enabled healthcheck.timer >/dev/null 2>&1 && timer_enabled='true'; log_ok='false'; [ -f /var/log/healthcheck.log ] && grep -q 'health_ok' /var/log/healthcheck.log && log_ok='true'; conf_ok='false'; [ -f /etc/healthcheck.conf ] && grep -q 'enabled=true' /etc/healthcheck.conf && grep -q 'interval=10m' /etc/healthcheck.conf && conf_ok='true'; echo "script_ok=$script_ok"; echo "svc_ok=$svc_ok"; echo "timer_ok=$timer_ok"; echo "timer_active=$timer_active"; echo "timer_enabled=$timer_enabled"; echo "log_ok=$log_ok"; echo "conf_ok=$conf_ok"
